segments.text: Shoe boxes are shaped like shoes. Phone boxes are shaped like phone. But pizza boxes, they are square. Pizzas are round. You think round pizza will get
round boxes. But here is what pizza companies discovered. Making the round boxes was costing them three times more money. A square box needs one sheet of
cardboard. Straight cuts, simple folds done in seconds. While a round one needs multiple pieces, curved cuts and special joining. And here is the killer part.
Every time you cut a circle in the cardboard, you waste everything around it. And square boxes waste almost nothing. Square boxes stack perfectly.
No gaps, no rolling, maximum space efficiency. Round boxes leave empty spaces everywhere. But here is the genius part most people miss. Those
corners are not wasted. They are the features. These act as the perfect spots for grabbing the pizza without even touching the toppings. Space for sauce
packets. Room for steam to escape. Lot of applications. Sometimes the best design isn't about matching the product. It's about optimizing manufacturing,
shipping, and customer experience. If you want more amazing design stories, subscribe.
